ground_truth.title: Gym Workouts for Beginners — Strength Training - Gold's Gym
ground_truth.main_content: There's nothing quite like taking the first step of your fitness journey. Whether you're looking for a life-changing transformation or want to hit a specific goal, strength training can help you get there. At Gold's Gym, we believe strength training is one of the best places to begin. Studies show that strength training helps increase lean mass and improve muscle strength. We put together this guide on beginner workouts to do at the gym with expert advice, step-by-step instructions, and top tips for maintaining long-term motivation. The guide covers why strength training is essential, how to prepare with a gym bag checklist, physical and mental preparation tips, and a complete beginner workout plan including warm-up, squats, push-ups, bent-over dumbbell rows, planks, dumbbell shoulder presses, lunges, glute bridges, and cool-down. Common mistakes to avoid include overtraining, improper form, skipping warm-ups and cool-downs, and never changing workouts. Tips for staying motivated include being proud to show up, being patient with results, and asking trainers for help.
with: There's nothing quite like taking the first step of your fitness journey.
Studies also show that strength training helps increase lean mass and improve muscle strength.
Squats: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Begin lowering down as if you were going to sit in a chair. Keep your chest up and knees behind your toes.
Push-ups: Start on all fours. Straighten your legs behind you and balance on your toes. Your hands should be just barely wider than shoulder-width apart.
Strength training is one of our favorite gym workouts for beginners because it targets all the major muscle groups in the body without overloading any one specific area.
without: Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information
